commit.message:
Fix CORS preflight error: use text/plain instead of application/json

Google Apps Script does not handle OPTIONS preflight requests (returns
405). Changed Content-Type to text/plain which avoids CORS preflight
since it's a "simple request". GAS doPost receives the body the same
way. Also added redirect: follow for GAS 302 redirects. SW v40.

Add browser and screen control via local agent

- Browser tools (Playwright): browser_navigate, browser_screenshot,
  browser_click, browser_type, browser_get_text, browser_evaluate,
  browser_back, browser_close — opens a visible browser window so
  you can watch Jarvis work

  SEE the page/screen and decide what to do next — real computer-use loop
- lib/ai/tool-result.ts: formatToolContent helper handles image results
  across chat, cron, and Jarvis 4 Pi chat routes
- agent/index.mjs: Playwright browser instance + macOS screencapture
- Install: npm install playwright && npx playwright install chromium

Fix sync error: boolean false is not a valid IDB key

getUnsyncedInterventions() was using getByIndex('synced', false) which
throws because IndexedDB does not support boolean keys. Changed to
filter all interventions in JS with !i.synced instead. SW v39.

fix: move canvas toolbar outside ReactFlow to fix dropdown clipping

ReactFlow's root element creates an isolated stacking context, trapping
the Add Step / Templates dropdown z-indices inside it. Panels rendered
later in the tree (empty-state hint) visually overlapped the menus.

Fix: render CanvasToolbar in a plain absolutely-positioned div that sits
outside <ReactFlow> but inside the same canvas container, at z-index 200.
All 7 node-type items now render without clipping.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix cloud save: debounceSave now triggers cloud save every 3s

iPad Safari doesn't reliably fire beforeunload, so the 30-second
setInterval was the only cloud save trigger. If it fired before data
was entered, it overwrote with empty state. Now every debounceSave
also queues a cloud save (3s debounce to avoid hammering).

Also fixes SESSION_DATE from 03-08 to 03-09.

commit.message:
Fix split tournament ranking: merge sub-tournaments by position pairing

When importing E2i match CSVs for split tournaments (A/B), each sub-tournament
is now ranked independently, then merged by pairing same positions with
tiebreaker: match points > moyenne > serie.

Also fixes E2i regex patterns to handle ampersand separators and parenthesized
formats (e.g. Classement 5 & 6, Classement (3 & 4), Finale (1 & 2)).

commit.message:
Save RF coverage via HTTP POST instead of socket to avoid size limits v0.52.0

The 11.5MB geojson payload (54k per-sample features) exceeded Socket.IO's
message size handling, causing the server-to-client response to fail silently.
Now saves via HTTP POST which handles large payloads properly. Client updates
its own store directly from the HTTP response. Also increased Express JSON
body limit from 10MB to 50MB.

commit.message:
perf: 3-4x faster embedding job — pipelining, larger batches, higher concurrency

- EMBEDDING_BATCH_SIZE: 10 → 40 (eliminate sequential sub-batching)
- Route batch sizes: 20→40 (images), 30→60 (text-only)
- Time budget: 180s → 240s (use 80% of maxDuration)
- Pipeline compute+write: overlap DB write with next Bedrock compute
- Add timing logs per batch for performance monitoring

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: phase 1 PoC — stateless emptyDir + RS=2 on canary-prod

Eliminates the shared cinder-rwx PVC that caused ext4 inode corruption
at RS=2 (confirmed 2026-03-09). With persistence.enabled: false each pod
gets its own emptyDir; no shared block device means RS=2 is safe.

- canary-overrides.yaml: persistence.enabled: false
- tenant-canary-prod.yaml: replicaCount: 2, remove persistence block

OpenSpec change: stateless-nextcloud-ha (phase 1 of 3)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

EPIC-047: Git workflow enforcement — commit discipline for governance work

Addresses IMPL-015 (237 files uncommitted across sessions). Root causes:
no commit-frequency enforcement, governance work exempt from worktrees,
session hooks blind to uncommitted file count.

Changes:
- session-start-hook.sh: warns when uncommitted files on main exceed 20
- pre-commit-reminder.sh: shows uncommitted count, adds commit discipline checklist
- git-workflow.md: new "Commit Discipline" section covering governance-only work,
  commit boundaries, and 20-file threshold
- project-type-software skill: commit discipline section for software projects
- IMPL-015 promoted to enforcement artifacts

Replace flat document list with category folder grid view

Documents now display as clickable folder cards organized by category.
Each folder shows the category name, document count, and a color-coded
left border. Click a folder to see its documents, click back to return
to the grid. An "All Documents" folder shows everything in a flat list.

All existing functionality preserved: download, delete, retry, status
badges, category badges, file size/date display.

Add "Publish Cards" feature — two-stage In Progress flow

Players cannot see card assignments until the admin explicitly publishes
them, allowing card setup to be finalized before revealing them.

- schema: add `cardsPublished Boolean @default(false)` to LeagueNight
- migration: 20260309000000_add_cards_published
- backend: expose `cardsPublished` as an updatable field on PATCH /admin/league-nights/:id
- frontend types: add `cardsPublished` to LeagueNight interface
- AdminCheckInPage: "📢 Publish Cards" / "✓ Published" toggle button in the Cards section header
- LeagueNightPage: gate "My Card" on `night.cardsPublished || isAdmin`; show "Cards are being finalized" placeholder to checked-in players when cards aren't published; show admin-only unpublished warning banner on the card
